basic_details.job_title: Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 1265 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Society for Centralized Recruitment of Staff in Subordinate Courts (S.S.S.C.) under High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh
basic_details.department: District and Sessions Courts across the State of Haryana
basic_details.total_vacancies: 1265 (1015 existing as on 31.12.2025 + 250 anticipated up to 30.06.2026)
basic_details.short_information: Haryana High Court is recruiting 1265 Clerk posts for Subordinate Courts of Haryana with Bachelor's degree qualification (BA/BSc or equivalent) and computer proficiency, last date 23 June 2026.

important_dates.qualification cut-off date: 23-06-2026 (closing date for submission of online applications)
important_dates.ews certificate validity required: Valid for FY 2026-2027, based on gross annual income for FY 2025-2026, issued on or before 23-06-2026
important_dates.demo / mock test availability: 15 days prior to date of examination (on www.sssc.gov.in)
important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on www.sssc.gov.in (exam can be held anywhere in Northern India)
important_dates.document verification / interaction date: To be notified after written exam and CPT results

application_fee.details: Fee is non-refundable and must be paid online only. The Online Application Form will be submitted only after payment of fees. Application fee for "Others" (Transgender) category is applicable as per their category in the fee table below.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01.01.2026. The upper age limit for any candidate (except Ex-servicemen) shall not exceed 52 years even after availing all applicable age relaxations combined. Reservation benefits of age relaxation are only for bonafide residents of Haryana.

qualification.domicile: As per Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997. Reservation benefits only for bonafide Haryana residents with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates.
qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Arts (BA) or Bachelor of Science (BSc) or equivalent degree from a recognized university. AND proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of application (23-06-2026). All particulars including category and sub-category once filled by the applicant in the Online Application Form shall be sacrosanct and no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process.

OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, LC, Dw, AAV
vacancy_details.Description: One Arm, Both Arms, One Leg, Both Legs, One Arm and One Leg, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims
Multiple Disabilities involving any combination of categories (a) to (d) above

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Multiple Disabilities involving any combination of categories (a) to (d) above

salary_details.pay_scale: As per latest rules and instructions of Government of Haryana duly adopted for Subordinate Courts of Haryana

selection_process: Sr. No. Subject Type No. of Questions Maximum Marks Qualifying Marks

1 General Knowledge Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 50 33% in each subject individually; AND minimum 40% aggregate of both subjects. English Composition (Subjective) checked only for candidates who score 33%+ in General Knowledge.
2 English Composition Objective (MCQ) 50 questions × 1 mark each 20
Subjective (typed on computer) i. Essay (150 words) 10
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply in online mode only at www.sssc.gov.in under the head "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana". No other means/mode of submission of application will be accepted. The online application form is submitted only after payment of fees. All applicants (including reserved category) can submit only one application per category - if more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered.
Before starting the Online Application Form, keep the following ready:
Personal details (Name, Date of Birth, Category, Sub-category etc.)
Valid and active Email ID (keep unchanged throughout the process - do not use email of unknown persons)
important_instructions: All applicants irrespective of their category (General or reserved) can submit only one application per category. If more than one application is submitted, only the latest application will be considered. All particulars including category and sub-category once filled in the Online Application Form are sacrosanct - no change shall be permitted at any stage of the recruitment process. Fill all details after taking due care and precaution. Reservation benefits (age, fee, category) are only for bonafide residents of Haryana with valid Haryana Government-issued certificates valid as on 23-06-2026. Candidates from other states' reserved categories must apply under General category. The photograph pasted on the E-Admit Card must be the same as uploaded in the Online Application Form. Mismatch will result in denial of entry at the examination centre. Facial appearance at the time of examination must match the photographs in the application and E-Admit Card - failing which candidature can be rejected at any stage. No TA/DA shall be paid for appearing in any stage of the selection process. No mobile phone, electronic device, watch, jewellery, or baggage material is allowed inside the examination centre. The decision of the High Court in all matters relating to acceptance/rejection of application, eligibility/suitability, mode/criteria for selection shall be final and binding. The High Court may shortlist candidates at any stage based on any criteria. Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date to avoid last-minute website congestion. The cut-off date is sacrosanct - no extension will be granted under any circumstance, and requests by post/fax/email/by hand will not be entertained. Mere satisfying eligibility criteria does not entitle an applicant to be called for examination. The High Court reserves the right to alter/modify or change any of the terms and conditions including selection criteria. Concealing any material fact or furnishing false information can result in rejection by the High Court at any stage. Canvassing in any form will entail cancellation of candidature. The candidature at all stages is purely provisional - it can be cancelled at any point if the applicant is found not meeting eligibility conditions, even after completion of the selection process. Success in the examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied that the candidate is suitable in all respects for appointment to the service. All communications to SSSC must invariably contain: Advertisement No. & Post Name, Registration ID, Roll No. (if received), Full Name in Block Letters, Registered Email ID, and Correspondence Address as given in the application form. In case of any discrepancy between the advertisement in newspapers and the SSSC website content, www.sssc.gov.in shall prevail. All notices, results, exam dates, and corrigendum will be notified on www.sssc.gov.in only - candidates must check the website and their email (inbox as well as spam box) regularly.
